author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XV. 4–7.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus called Himself the vine, and His disciples the branches, and His Father the husbandman; whereon we have already discoursed as we were able.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in the present passage, while still speaking of Himself as the vine, and of His branches, or, in other words, of the disciples, He said, “Abide in me, and I in you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are not in Him in the same kind of way that He is in them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet both ways tend to their advantage, and not to His.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the relation of the branches to the vine is such that they contribute nothing to the vine, but from it derive their own means of life; while that of the vine to the branches is such that it supplies their vital nourishment, and receives nothing from them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so their having Christ abiding in them, and abiding themselves in Christ, are in both respects advantageous, not to Christ, but to the disciples.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the branch is cut off, another may spring up from the living root; but that which is cut off cannot live apart from the root.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then He proceeds to say: “As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A great encomium on grace, my brethren,—one that will instruct the souls of the humble, and stop the mouths of the proud.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let those now answer it, if they dare, who, ignorant of God’s righteousness, and going about to establish their own, have not submitted themselves unto the righteousness of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the self-complacent answer it, who think they have no need of God for the performance of good works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Fight they not against such a truth, those men of corrupt mind, reprobate concerning the faith, whose reply is only full of impious talk, when they say: It is of God that we have our existence as men, but it is of ourselves that we are righteous?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it you say, you who deceive yourselves, and, instead of establishing freewill, cast it headlong down from the heights of its self-elevation through the empty regions of presumption into the depths of an ocean grave?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, your assertion that man of himself worketh righteousness, that is the height of your self-elation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Truth contradicts you, and declares, “The branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Away with you now over your giddy precipices, and, without a spot whereon to take your stand, vapor away at your windy talk.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These are the empty regions of your presumption.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But look well at what is tracking your steps, and, if you have any sense remaining, let your hair stand on end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For whoever imagines that he is bearing fruit of himself is not in the vine, and he that is not in the vine is not in Christ, and he that is not in Christ is not a Christian.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such are the ocean depths into which you have plunged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ponder again and again what the Truth has still further to say: “I am the vine,” He adds, “ye are the branches: he that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit; for without me ye can do nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For just to keep any from supposing that the branch can bear at least some little fruit of itself, after saying, “the same bringeth forth much fruit,” His next words are not, Without me ye can do but little, but “ye can do nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whether then it be little or much, without Him it is impracticable; for without Him nothing can be done.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For although, when the branch beareth little fruit, the husbandman purgeth it that it may bring forth more; yet if it abide not in the vine, and draw its life from the root, it can bear no fruit whatever of itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And although Christ would not have been the vine had He not been man, yet He could not have supplied such grace to the branches had He not also been God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And just because such grace is so essential to life, that even death itself ceases to be at the disposal of free-will, He adds, “If any one abide not in me, he shall be cast forth as a branch, and wither; and they shall gather him, and cast him into the fire, and he is burned.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The wood of the vine, therefore, is in the same proportion the more contemptible if it abide not in the vine, as it is glorious while so abiding; in fine, as the Lord likewise says of them in the prophet Ezekiel, when cut off, they are of no use for any purpose of the husbandman, and can be applied to no labor of the mechanic.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The branch is suitable only for one of two things, either the vine or the fire: if it is not in the vine, its place will be in the fire; and that it may escape the latter, may it have its place in the vine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If ye abide in me,” He says, “and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For abiding thus in Christ, is there aught they can wish but what will be agreeable to Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So abiding in the Saviour, can they wish anything that is inconsistent with salvation?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some things, indeed, we wish because we are in Christ, and other things we desire because still in this world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For at times, in connection with this our present abode, we are inwardly prompted to ask what we know not it would be inexpedient for us to receive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But God forbid that such should be given us if we abide in Christ, who, when we ask, only does what will be for our advantage.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Abiding, therefore, ourselves in Him, when His words abide in us we shall ask what we will, and it shall be done unto us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if we ask, and the doing follows not, what we ask is not connected with our abiding in Him, nor with His words which abide in us, but with that craving and infirmity of the flesh which are not in Him, and have not His words abiding in them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to His words, at all events, belongs that prayer which He taught, and in which we say, “Our Father, who art in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us only not fall away from the words and meaning of this prayer in our petitions, and whatever we ask, it shall be done unto us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For then only may His words be said to abide in us, when we do what He has commanded us, and love what He has promised.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when His words abide only in the memory, and have no place in the life, the branch is not to be accounted as in the vine, because it draws not its life from the root.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is to this distinction that the word of Scripture has respect, “and to those that remember His commandments to do them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For many retain them in their memory only to treat them with contempt, or even to mock at and assail them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not in such as have only some kind of contact, but no connection, that the words of Christ abide; and to them, therefore, they will not be a blessing, but a testimony against them; and because they are present in them without abiding in them, they are held fast by them for the very purpose of being judged according to them at last.
